cases.description: Developer asks to learn from the last AI-generated commit
cases.prompt: I just vibe coded a checkout refactor with Claude. Debrief me on what I built from the last commit and quiz me so I actually learn it.
assertions: skill activates and asks for or inspects the concrete git diff/last commit
report starts from changed files and commit facts, not chat transcript
explains 3-7 highest-learning-value files rather than every file mechanically
every named pattern or concept includes a file/function reference
includes caveats for edge cases, performance, security/privacy, and tech debt
quiz questions are practical and specific to the diff

cases.description: User wants a code review, not a learning debrief
cases.prompt: Please review this PR for bugs and approve whether it can deploy.
assertions: skill does NOT activate as the primary workflow
response routes to code review rather than producing a learning quiz
does not treat deploy approval as part of the debrief output

cases.description: User asks for a debrief but provides no repository or diff
cases.prompt: Explain what I just built from our chat so I can learn it.
assertions: skill refuses to debrief from chat alone
asks for git diff, commit range, PR URL, or repository access
does not invent architecture decisions or patterns
